body:
DESIGN-STAGE (Myk's merge, P6 — it fixes ecosystem conventions). ORIGIN (Myk, 2026-07-24, in chat): 'it seems wildly unlikely this tool takes off without some sort of schema discovery mechanism' — a public aggregator (loamhub) where you find the highest-rated Post schema and pull it into your own store, and where an app's manifest declares it uses (or precisely mirrors) that schema so compatibility in the Post domain is knowable up front.

THE THESIS, and why this is cheap: a hub is not new architecture. It is a Loam store whose ground is schemas, manifests, module versions, and RATING deltas; discovery is federation plus resolved views ('highest-rated Post schema' is a view over rating claims); publishing to the hub is federating deltas at it. What this ticket designs is the CONVENTIONS, not machinery:

(1) RATINGS AS DELTAS — the rating vocabulary (who rated what, at which version address), so reputation is data with authorship and trust context, resolvable under any policy a reader prefers (your hub view need not equal mine).
(2) MIRRORING/PINNING — the manifest convention for 'my Post IS post-schema@X' (verifiable by address equality at the §21 schema-identity level, which SURVIVES adoption re-signing) vs 'my module PINS post-schema@X' (§27.4 frozen DAG). Compatibility as arithmetic: two artifacts interop in the Post domain iff they resolve Post through the same address, checkable before any install.
(3) BRIDGES — when addresses genuinely differ, published adapter modules make two schemas functionally interchangeable, across THREE axes (2026-07-24 riff): VOCABULARY (a reading that gathers both shapes — reconciliation as a lens, nothing rewritten), SEMANTICS (a derived function emitting one shape from the other, each emission carrying derivation provenance, replay-verifiable), and IDENTITY (same-as claims mapping entity ids at a federation boundary — signed, trust-scoped, and STRIKEABLE, which is what keeps same-as from the transitive-misidentification swamp). A bridge module's manifest declares source address, target address, and COVERAGE (what it preserves; bridges may be lossy/partial, and chained bridges compound losses visibly). The compat relation is then a GRAPH: nodes are schema addresses, free edges are identity, published edges are bridges; 'functionally interchangeable' = reachable, with the manifest chain stating what survives the path. Adopting a bridge is adopting LAW — T31's per-export gesture, unchanged.
(4) FEDERATION-BETWEEN-STRANGERS — the same bridge modules are how two instances with entirely different semantics or different primary keys federate: the bridge sits at the boundary, the identity mapping is preserved as claims (Myk, 2026-07-24).

DESIGN QUESTIONS for the working spec: the rating vocabulary's shape (per-version vs per-export ratings — recommendation: per-export, matching discovery's unit); whether a hub needs any privileged role at all (recommendation: no — a hub is a store like any other, and 'official' is a trust decision each consumer makes); bridge-coverage vocabulary (enumerated preserved fields vs a free-text claim — recommendation: enumerated, so coverage composes mechanically along a path).

Evidence: the 2026-07-24 design conversation; spec/27-containers.md §27.8 (manifest, mirroring); spec/28-container-trust.md substrate note (the canonical published Post body is 'the shape the substrate encourages'); src/federation/ translator door (T54) as the embryo of boundary translation.

edges.contract: The hub's one-gesture adopt-by-address IS T31's blessing applied at the discovery edge; the two must land as one shape or the ecosystem gets two install verbs.

title: The hub — discovery, ratings-as-deltas, mirroring, and BRIDGES across vocabulary, semantics, and identity (new SPEC §29)
